interest: Equity advocates who want the system to protect the weakest students by keeping mixed-ability cohorts intact, so no cohort loses its peer resources through early sorting.
value: Equity (peer-effect gains for weaker students) in tension with the practical capacity of receiving schools to absorb them without diluting excellence.
fear: That a well-intentioned reduction in selective places, if rushed, dumps weaker students into schools unprepared to support them, converting a hoped-for equity gain into a shared decline.
affected: stronger peers who remain in mixed cohorts
assumption: That positive peer effects from keeping weaker students alongside stronger ones are real and net-positive, and that support for receiving schools can realistically be scaled to match any chosen phase-down pace.
empirical_uncertainty: Contested: peer-effect evidence exists but its magnitude and direction depend heavily on implementation, and the pace-vs-support threshold is unquantified, making the conditional claim's factual core unsettled.
